findings.evidence: `code/.../Deck.lean:66` defines the equivalence by hand; Mathlib has `Homeomorph.subtype` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Topology/Homeomorph/Lemmas.lean:160` and the underlying `Equiv.subtypeEquiv` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Logic/Equiv/Basic.lean:262`.
findings.fix: Define it using `φ.1.toEquiv.subtypeEquiv` or, preferably with the stronger topological API after importing `Mathlib.Topology.Homeomorph.Lemmas`, `(φ.1.subtype (p := fun e => p e = b) (q := fun e => p e = b) (by intro e; simp [map_proj φ e])).toEquiv`, keeping only the deck-specific wrapper and simp lemmas if needed.
findings.issue: `Deck.fiberEquiv` manually constructs the restriction of a homeomorphism/equivalence to an invariant fibre, duplicating the generic subtype API.

summary: The deck subgroup itself does not duplicate an existing Mathlib/TauCeti declaration, but the fibre restriction equivalence reimplements Mathlib's generic subtype-restriction API.
